1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/d3d640f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3779x3779+944+0/resize/600x600!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe1%2Fec%2Fbc18e977482487da60c17695cee1%2Fjackson-030726-73.JPG 2x" width="300" height="300" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/a28a221/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3779x3779+944+0/resize/300x300!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe1%2Fec%2Fbc18e977482487da60c17695cee1%2Fjackson-030726-73.JPG" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIzMDBweCIgd2lkdGg9IjMwMHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> </span> </div> </div> </div></div><div class="FlickityCustomNavigation-wrapper"> <div class="FlickityCustomNavigation below"> <button class="FlickityCustomNavigation-button-prev" type="button" aria-label="Previous"> <svg class="FlickityCustomNavigation-button-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-left"></use></svg> </button> <button class="FlickityCustomNavigation-button-next" type="button" aria-label="Next"> <svg class="FlickityCustomNavigation-button-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-right"></use></svg> </button> </div> </div></div> </bsp-carousel> </div> </div><p></p><h3><b>‘Eres alguien’</b></h3><p>Isiah Thomas, miembro del Salón de la Fama del equipo de basketball Detroit Pistons, recordó haber crecido en la década de 1960 admirando a Jesse Jackson como un héroe de los derechos civiles.</p><p>“Acabábamos de salir de las leyes de Jim Crow”, comentó Thomas. “Cuando la sociedad me decía que no era nadie, cuando la sociedad me decía que ni siquiera queríamos ir a la escuela contigo, este hombre se acercó a mí y a mi madre... e hizo lo impensable. Mamá Jackson, tu esposo se arrodilló, me miró a los ojos y me dijo: ‘Eres alguien’”.</p><p>Thomas lloró al recordar cómo Jackson consoló a su familia cuando falleció su madre. “Despidió a mi mamá con hermosas palabras. Cuando estábamos en nuestro peor momento, nos levantó sobre sus hombros”.</p><p>La celebración de los 84 años de vida de Jackson tuvo un gran estilo, con muchos asistentes ataviados con sus mejores galas o el uniforme de funeral. Pero también hubo algunas gorras de béisbol, y Mr. T., la estrella de televisión de los 80, estuvo allí con un overol con estampado la bandera estadounidense.</p><p>El gobernador J.B. Pritzker también habló, comenzando su discurso citando la Biblia: “Que fluya la justicia como las aguas, y la rectitud como un impetuoso arroyo. Para el reverendo Jesse Jackson, cada día era una nueva oportunidad para hacer justicia en un mundo a menudo injusto. Su ambición era forjar un mundo donde la justicia no fuera una anomalía, sino una constante”.</p><p>“Mientras lo compartíamos con el mundo, el reverendo Jackson pertenecía a Chicago, y Chicago le pertenecía a él”, dijo el gobernador.</p><p>Biden también recurrió a las Escrituras, diciendo: “Jesse creía que la Biblia nos dice que quienes esperan en el Señor renovarán sus fuerzas”, dijo Biden. “Levantarán alas de águila. Correrán y no se cansarán”.</p><p>Johnson llamó a Jackson “un brillante estratega, un sabio organizador”.</p><p>“Fueron sus instintos los que comprendieron que el trabajo y la fe eran uno y lo mismo, como profetizó el Dr. King. Fueron sus instintos los que lo impulsaron a oponerse al cierre de escuelas y clínicas de salud mental. Fueron sus instintos los que impulsaron las protestas en la Michigan Avenue cuando un policía le quitó la vida a un niño y el gobierno intentó encubrirlo”. Su instinto le llevó a postularse en 1984 y en 1988, pero no en 1992, para dar paso a alguien del Sur Profundo y a un hermano del Sur para convertirse en presidente de Estados Unidos”, dijo Johnson, refiriéndose a Clinton y Obama.</p><p>También hubo humor. El rabino Steven Jacobs bromeó sobre la llegada de Jackson a las Puertas del Cielo y el encuentro con Dios, quien exclamó: “¡Dios mío, eres Jesse Jackson! ¡Eres alguien!”.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-f80000" name="image-f80000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/a19d612/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4431x2487+0+234/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe4%2F28%2F05f639644e9abf3f3eddd9bc2bce%2Fjackson-030726-039.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6993ce2/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4431x2487+0+234/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe4%2F28%2F05f639644e9abf3f3eddd9bc2bce%2Fjackson-030726-039.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/1935ff9/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4431x2487+0+234/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe4%2F28%2F05f639644e9abf3f3eddd9bc2bce%2Fjackson-030726-039.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Jennifer Hudson cantó durante el funeral del viernes en la House of Hope, en el lado sur. " srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/1935ff9/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4431x2487+0+234/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe4%2F28%2F05f639644e9abf3f3eddd9bc2bce%2Fjackson-030726-039.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f8c6239/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4431x2487+0+234/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe4%2F28%2F05f639644e9abf3f3eddd9bc2bce%2Fjackson-030726-039.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/1935ff9/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4431x2487+0+234/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe4%2F28%2F05f639644e9abf3f3eddd9bc2bce%2Fjackson-030726-039.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Jennifer Hudson cantó durante el funeral del viernes en la House of Hope, en el lado sur.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Candace Dane Chambers/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p></p><h3><b>‘Un hermoso recuerdo’</b></h3><p>El presidente Donald Trump fue invitado, pero no asistió, al igual que el otro presidente vivo, George W. Bush.</p><p>Harris, una de las últimas oradoras, describió la celebración del viernes de la vida y el legado de Jackson como “un hermoso recuerdo de su espíritu, su vida y su fe”.</p><p>La ex vicepresidenta dijo que el día había renovado “su fe en lo que es posible”.</p><p>“De niña, me criaron con la idea de que cuando ves una puerta cerrada debes tocar y esperar a que te inviten a entrar. Lo que la vida me ha enseñado es que si no se te ofrece esa invitación y esa puerta permanece cerrada después de repetidos intentos de tocarla, a veces no tienes más remedio que abrirla de una patada”, compartió Harris. </p><p>“El reverendo Jackson era impaciente. No perdía el tiempo esperando, ni siquiera cuando las puertas frente a él estaban cerradas con llave... Siempre encontraba la manera de entrar”.</p><p><i>Contribuyó: Tina Sfondeles</i></p><p><i>Traducido y editado por </i><a class="Link" href="suntimes.com/lavoz" target="_blank" ><i>La Voz Chicago</i></a></p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-140000" name="module-140000"></a> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody"><ul id="rte-2f5a1752-1354-11f1-9468-4ffaee5f793a"><li><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/news/2026/02/18/friends-and-family-say-chicago-embraced-and-nurtured-civil-rights-icon-jesse-jackson-from-the-start" >Friends and family say Chicago embraced and nurtured civil rights icon Jesse Jackson from the start</a></li><li><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/politics/2026/02/17/rev-jesse-jackson-chicago-mayors-a-fraught-and-contentious-relationship" >Rev. Jesse Jackson and Chicago mayors — a tense, fraught relationship</a></li><li><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/education/2026/02/21/how-jesse-jackson-shaped-chicago-education-from-protesting-school-segregation-to-reopening-dyett-hs" >How Jesse Jackson shaped Chicago education, from protesting school segregation to reopening Dyett HS</a></li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.wbez.org/in-the-loop-with-sasha-ann-simons/2026/02/19/how-the-late-rev-jesse-jackson-was-a-pioneer-of-the-progressive-movement-and-lgbtq-ally" >Listen: How the late Rev. Jesse Jackson was a pioneer of the progressive movement and LGBTQ+ ally</a></li><li><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/money/2026/02/20/jesse-jackson-economic-employment-justice-boycott-civil-rights" >For Jesse Jackson, economic justice and civil rights were intertwined</a></li><li><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/columnists/2026/02/18/lights-camera-jesse-jackson-civil-rights-leader-pop-culture-presence" >Lights, camera, Jackson: Civil Rights leader also became a pop culture presence</a></li></ul></div> </div><p></p>
entries.author: Neil Steinberg

entries.title: Bears' 2026 NFL draft order set
entries.summary: <p>One day after announcing <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/nfl-finalizes-compensatory-draft-picks-bears-get-none-for-falcons-hiring-ian-cunningham" target="_blank" >compensatory picks</a>, the NFL on Tuesday put out the official order for the April draft in Pittsburgh.</p><p>The <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears" target="_blank" >Bears</a> have three picks in the first 60 — No. 25 in the first round, No. 57 in the second and, once the DJ Moore trade to the Bills is made official Wednesday, Buffalo’s No. 60 overall.</p><p>The Bears have picks in Rounds 3, 4 and 5, and two in Round 7. The Bears previously traded a fourth-round pick to the Chiefs but acquired one from the Rams on draft day last year. They traded their sixth-rounder to the Browns for defensive end Joe Tryon-Shoyinka and a seventh-rounder in November.</p><p>They do not have a compensatory third-round pick despite appealing to the league in the wake of assistant general manager Ian Cunningham taking the Falcons' GM job.</p><p>Here’s the full list of Bears selections:<br>Round 1, Pick 25<br>Round 2, Pick 57<br>Round 2, Pick 30 via Bills (to be made official Wednesday)<br>Round 3, Pick 89<br>Round 4, Pick 129 via Rams<br>Round 5, Pick 165<br>Round 7, Pick 239 via Eagles through Jaguars and Browns<br>Round 7, Pick 241<br></p>
entries.author: Patrick Finley

entries.title: Bears to sign former Lions wide receiver, returner Kalif Raymond: source
entries.summary: <p>The <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears" target="_blank" >Bears</a> are looking for wide receivers to compete behind Rome Odunze and Luther Burden this season, and longtime Lions player Kalif Raymond is in the mix. The Bears agreed to a one-year, $5.1 million deal with Raymond on Tuesday, a source said, and he will sign when contracts can be finalized Wednesday.</p><p>Raymond, 31, joined the Bears a day after Olamide Zaccheaus left for the Falcons in free agency. He and Jahdae Walker currently are next in line after Odunze and Burden.</p><p>Raymond had 24 catches for 289 yards and a touchdown for the Lions last season, as well as returning punts and kickoffs. He had a punt return for a touchdown last season and has three in his career.</p><p>He was with the Lions the last five seasons and overlapped with Bears coach <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2025/09/01/the-bears-and-their-fans-needed-a-truth-teller-they-got-one-in-new-coach-ben-johnson" >Ben Johnson</a> from 2021 through '24. He had his best seasons when Johnson was a Lions assistant, and over those four seasons he totaled 147 receptions, 1,896 yards and seven touchdowns.</p>
entries.author: Jason Lieser

entries.title: Review: ‘Hamilton’ remains crashingly relevant in return to Chicago’s CIBC Theatre
entries.summary: <p>It’s been 11 years since I first saw <a class="Link" href="https://www.broadwayinchicago.com/shows/hamilton-2/" target="_blank" ><u>“Hamilton,”</u></a> Lin-Manuel Miranda’s trailblazing Tony Award-winning musical. It feels like several lifetimes and another country ago. What’s remained unimpeachable through the crashing tumult of recent history is the show’s ability to amaze and astonish.</p><p>Running through April 26 at the Loop’s CIBC Theatre, the musical (with its book, score and lyrics by Miranda) follows Ron Chernow’s door-stop biography of immigrant and Founding Father Alexander Hamilton. The tale it spins of the messy birth of a nation hasn’t aged since its 2015 premiere.</p><p>Directed by Thomas Kail, the production hurtles toward the climatic duel between the “immigrant bastard son of a whore” Hamilton (Tyler Fauntleroy) and “the damn fool that shot him,” Aaron Burr (Jimmie “JJ“ Jeter).</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-hangRight> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-fc0000" name="image-fc0000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/04a6ee4/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3725x2091+0+1749/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa9%2Fd7%2F8e77ef3747e8ace169f4c8cd036d%2Fl-r-tyler-fauntleroy-and-jimmie-j-j-jeter-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/ffa9faf/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3725x2091+0+1749/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa9%2Fd7%2F8e77ef3747e8ace169f4c8cd036d%2Fl-r-tyler-fauntleroy-and-jimmie-j-j-jeter-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/274d1ca/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3725x2091+0+1749/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa9%2Fd7%2F8e77ef3747e8ace169f4c8cd036d%2Fl-r-tyler-fauntleroy-and-jimmie-j-j-jeter-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="(l-r) Tyler Fauntleroy and Jimmie J.J. Jeter - HAMILTON National Tour - (c) Joan Marcus 2024.jpg" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/274d1ca/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3725x2091+0+1749/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa9%2Fd7%2F8e77ef3747e8ace169f4c8cd036d%2Fl-r-tyler-fauntleroy-and-jimmie-j-j-jeter-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/2f7caf8/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3725x2091+0+1749/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa9%2Fd7%2F8e77ef3747e8ace169f4c8cd036d%2Fl-r-tyler-fauntleroy-and-jimmie-j-j-jeter-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/274d1ca/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3725x2091+0+1749/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa9%2Fd7%2F8e77ef3747e8ace169f4c8cd036d%2Fl-r-tyler-fauntleroy-and-jimmie-j-j-jeter-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>The production hurtles toward a dramatic duel between Hamilton (Tyler Fauntleroy, at left) and Aaron Burr (Jimmie J.J. Jeter).</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Photo by Joan Marcus </p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>By the time “Hamilton” arrives at that fatal encounter in Weehawken, New Jersey, the musical has immersed the audience in a celebratory, hope-infused story about the power of conviction, and the fact that even a “rag-tag army” army reduced to eating its horses can take down a global super power in the name of democracy.</p><p>Miranda’s score (orchestrations by Alex Lacamoire) encompasses rap, hip-hop, ’50s-style do-wop, rock, killer ballads and old-timey vaudeville, plus nods to the Notorius B.I.G., Gilbert and Sullivan, Rodgers and Hammerstein and Jason Robert Brown.</p><p>Still, the book and lyrics don’t pull any punches in pointing out the intractable contradiction of a country where the foundational premise that “all men are created equal” only applied only to white men. Most of the signers of the Declaration of Independence and roughly half the delegates to the Constitutional Convention owned slaves. The topic is a mic-drop flashpoint when Hamilton and Thomas Jefferson (Christian Magby, who also plays the Marquis de Lafayette) go mano-a-mano at a Constitutional Convention rap battle.</p><p>The performances feel as fresh as they were over a decade ago. A calling card of the musical since its original premiere, Hamilton, Burr and the cast of slave-owning Founders Washington, Jefferson and Madison are all portrayed by men of color. It’s a choice that adds layers on layers.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-960000" name="image-960000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/61ebc9f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5203x2920+0+260/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F61%2F4a%2F9a0c72194038bfc5d47e4a0aff67%2Fl-r-nathan-haydel-tyler-fauntleroy-jared-howelton-elvie-ellis-and-company-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/31f32a0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5203x2920+0+260/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F61%2F4a%2F9a0c72194038bfc5d47e4a0aff67%2Fl-r-nathan-haydel-tyler-fauntleroy-jared-howelton-elvie-ellis-and-company-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/fbe5db3/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5203x2920+0+260/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F61%2F4a%2F9a0c72194038bfc5d47e4a0aff67%2Fl-r-nathan-haydel-tyler-fauntleroy-jared-howelton-elvie-ellis-and-company-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="(l-r) Nathan Haydel, Tyler Fauntleroy, Jared Howelton, Elvie Ellis, and Company - HAMILTON National Tour - (c) Joan Marcus 2024.jpg" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/fbe5db3/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5203x2920+0+260/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F61%2F4a%2F9a0c72194038bfc5d47e4a0aff67%2Fl-r-nathan-haydel-tyler-fauntleroy-jared-howelton-elvie-ellis-and-company-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/56201d5/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5203x2920+0+260/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F61%2F4a%2F9a0c72194038bfc5d47e4a0aff67%2Fl-r-nathan-haydel-tyler-fauntleroy-jared-howelton-elvie-ellis-and-company-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/fbe5db3/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5203x2920+0+260/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F61%2F4a%2F9a0c72194038bfc5d47e4a0aff67%2Fl-r-nathan-haydel-tyler-fauntleroy-jared-howelton-elvie-ellis-and-company-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>“Hamilton” immerses audiences in a celebratory, hope-infused story about the power of conviction. Pictured here are actors (from left) Nathan Haydel, Tyler Fauntleroy, Jared Howelton and Elvie Ellis.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Photo by Joan Marcus </p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><div class="RatingCard Enhancement" data-module data-rating="3.5" data-align-center> <div class="RatingCard-title"> "Hamilton" </div> <div class="RatingCard-content"> <span class="RatingCard-star"> <span class="RatingCard-star-full"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star-half"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star"> <span class="RatingCard-star-full"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star-half"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star"> <span class="RatingCard-star-full"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star-half"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star"> <span class="RatingCard-star-full"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star-half"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> </span> </div> </div><p></p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-100000" name="module-100000"></a> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody"><b>When:</b> Through April 26<br><b>Where:</b> CIBC Theatre, 18 W. Monroe<br><b>Info:</b> <a class="Link" href="https://www.broadwayinchicago.com/shows/hamilton-2/" target="_blank" >Tickets are $63- $208;</a> run time is 2 hours, 45 minutes including one intermission</div> </div><p>Some of the more lightning-quick vocals aren’t as clear as they need to be — whether that’s a sound problem or a cast problem, it’s a bit frustrating. Still, for the record, this cast stacks up well against the original, star-studded ensemble.</p><p>Fauntleroy gives Hamilton galvanic, powerhouse vocals and a swagger matched only by his intelligence. “My Shot” is an indelible earworm and a goose-bump raising call to seize the day, even when the world has turned upside down.</p><p>Jeter lays bare Burr’s striving, needy ambition. With “Wait for It,” the money notes feel like they could reach the Harlem neighborhood where Hamilton lived with his wife Eliza (Amanda Simone Lee, in for Lauren Mariasoosay) and children. “The Room Where It Happens” has always been a table-jumping banger. Jeter nails the emotions powering the words; You can see his eyes turn to stone, moving from pleading to vengeful in the space of a verse.</p><p>Lafayette spits some of the fastest raps in “Hamilton,” notably in “Guns and Ships.” Magby nails it, making military strategy a subject of frenetic, can’t-look-away intensity.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-e40000" name="image-e40000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="(l-r) Lily Soto, Marja Harmon, Lauren Mariasoosay - HAMILTON National Tour - (c) Joan Marcus 2024.jpg" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/0f096d5/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5604x3145+0+380/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fd9%2Fe1%2F07543ac14762b8e502a21f658973%2Fl-r-lily-soto-marja-harmon-lauren-mariasoosay-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/aed484a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5604x3145+0+380/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fd9%2Fe1%2F07543ac14762b8e502a21f658973%2Fl-r-lily-soto-marja-harmon-lauren-mariasoosay-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/0f096d5/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5604x3145+0+380/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fd9%2Fe1%2F07543ac14762b8e502a21f658973%2Fl-r-lily-soto-marja-harmon-lauren-mariasoosay-hamilton-national-tour-c-joan-marcus-2024.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Lily Soto, Marja Harmon and Lauren Mariasoosay portray the Schuyler sisters, who bring beauty and thunder throughout the production. </p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Photo by Joan Marcus </p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>A.D. Weaver reads as 10 feet tall as he struts onto the stage as George Washington, apropos for a playing a man who was 6 feet 2 inches at a time when most men were 5 feet 7 inches at most. With a basso profundo that oozes authoritative energy, he’s a magnetic, towering presence. “One Last Time” is a beautiful gut-wrencher, about the peaceful transition of power. Weaver gives it an aching, splendid melancholy.</p><p>The Schuyler sisters —Hamilton’s wife Eliza and her sisters, Angelica and Peggy — do not disappoint. As Eliza, Lee brings anger and devastation to “Burn,” giving the melody a spun-glass beauty and a steely determination. Marja Harmon brings the thunder to Angelica Schuyler, particularly at the ball where Hamilton initially meets the sisters. Lily Soto plays the lesser-seen sister Peggy, as well as Maria Reynolds, a key figure in a blackmailing scheme to which Hamilton falls prey. “Say No to This” is Soto’s moment to shine with an irresistibly seductive siren song.</p><p>Last but hardly least: King George III. Matt Bittner makes George hilarious until he’s menacing until he’s mad. In all forms, he’s a highlight.</p><p>Andy Blankenbuehler’s choreography remains mesmerizing, from the vaudevillian shenanigans of “What’d I Miss” (a hilariously apropos choice to open the second act, when a few people always come in late) to the orbiting chairs and circling bodies that render the hurricane that left Hamilton an orphan.</p><p>Paul Tazewell’s costumes speak volumes: Hamilton dons a new green vest once he comes into money; Eliza’s gown is Madonna-blue; Lafayette’s puce suit is a small masterpiece of splendid dandyism.</p><p>Hamilton is a lot. Every moment of it remains stellar.</p><p>Here’s a tip from someone who has seen the show 15 times: Get the Hamilton app, play the ticket lottery for <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/theater/2026/02/11/hamilton-chicago-how-to-get-lottery-tickets" target="_blank" ><u>a shot at $10 seats.</u></a> You might end up in the tip-top of the balcony, you might end up in the front row of the orchestra. Either way, “Hamilton” stays gold.</p><p><i>Corrected: This story was updated to reflect that Amanda Simone Lee portrayed Eliza on opening night of "Hamilton" in Chicago.</i> </p>
entries.author: Catey Sullivan | For the Sun-Times

entries.title: Bears transaction tracker: Who's coming, going this offseason
entries.summary: <p>The <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears" target="_blank" >Bears</a> have been busy with a flurry of moves as they try to sharpen their roster — and make prudent salary cap decisions. </p><p>They can start agreeing to deals with free agents Monday, though players can't officially sign their contracts until the start of the league year Wednesday.</p><p>Here's a look at who the Bears have gained, and lost: </p><h3>March 10</h3><p>The Bears agreed to a one-year, $5.1 million deal with former Lions wide receiver and return man <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/10/bears-to-sign-former-lions-wide-receiver-returner-kalif-raymond-source" ><b>Kalif Raymond</b></a>. He had 24 catches for 289 yards and a touchdown for the Lions last season.</p><h3>March 9</h3><p>Third-string quarterback <b>Case Keenum</b>, who was a key part of Caleb Williams’ development last year under coach Ben Johnson, is coming back to the Bears, a source confirmed. He’ll make as $5.5 million and as much as $8 million over two years, per ESPN.</p><p>Keenum hasn't thrown a pass since 2023 but has been part of the growth of both Williams and Texans quarterback C.J. Stroud late in his career.</p><p>“He has been an integral part of what we’ve done,” Johnson said in January, days after the Bears’ emotional playoff win against the Packers. “He has been tremendous in terms of Caleb’s growth. That was really the thought process of bringing him in, is: the guy with skins on the wall, he has played in big games himself on a high level.</p><p>"He has won big games, he has lost big games ... it’s something that all these young players can really lean into, particularly in weeks like this where you come off an emotional win and yet you got to turn your sights on the next page pretty quickly."</p><p>After one of the best seasons of his career, former <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears" target="_blank" >Bears</a> linebacker <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/former-bears-linebacker-tremaine-edmunds-gets-3-year-36-million-deal-from-giants-report" ><b>Tremaine Edmunds</b></a> got a big contract offer from the Giants. He agreed Monday to a three-year, $36 million deal, sources confirmed to the Sun-Times. It includes $24 million guaranteed.</p><p>Former Bears defensive end <b>Dominique Robinson</b>, a fifth-round pick in 2022, is signing with the Texans on a one-year, $4 milliion deal, ESPN reported.</p><p>The Bears will sign linebacker <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/bears-to-sign-playmaking-veteran-devin-bush-jr-as-they-rebuild-linebacker-corps-source" ><b>Devin Bush Jr.</b> </a>from the Browns to a three-year, $30 million deal after the departure of Edmunds, a source said. Bush, a former No. 10 overall pick, had three interceptions and two forced fumbles last season.</p><p>Former Colts defensive tackle <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/bears-first-move-in-free-agency-is-deal-with-dt-neville-gallimore-source" ><b>Neville Gallimore</b></a> is joining the Bears on a two-year, $12 million deal when the new league year opens, a source said. He played all 17 games in Indianapolis last season, including eight starts.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">Bears Nation let’s do it!!!! Thank you for the opportunity. Let’s work! <a href="https://twitter.com/hashtag/BearDown?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">#BearDown</a> 🐻</p>— Neville Gallimore (@Path2Greatwork) <a href="https://twitter.com/Path2Greatwork/status/2031059927179972657?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">March 9, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>Linebacker <b>D'Marco Jackson</b> is returning to the Bears on a two-year, $7.5 million deal, a source confirmed. The Bears claimed Jackson on cut day during training camp, citing his experience in coordinator Dennis Allen's system while in New Orleans. He proved to be a solid special teams player and a worthy spot starter. In Week 15, he was named NFC Defensive Player of the Week after logging the first sack and interception of his career in a win against the Browns. </p><p>Receiver <b>Olamide Zaccheaus</b> is leaving the Bears for Atlanta, a source confirmed, agreeing to sign a contract with the Falcons. Zaccheaus spent one year in Chicago after helping the Commanders develop quarterback Jayden Daniels. In Chicago, he struggled with drops — his six were tied for 13<sup>th</sup>-most in the NFL — and by the end of the season was behind DJ Moore, Rome Odunze and Luther Burden on the Bears’ depth chart. He finished with 30 catches for 315 yards. In Atlanta, he’ll be reunited with new general manager Ian Cunningham, who was the Bears’ assistant GM last season. Zaccheaus played in Atlanta from 2019-22, catching passes from new president of football Matt Ryan, the former quarterback.</p><p>With none of their top four safeties under contract for this upcoming season, the Bears needed to move quickly to grab capable players at the position. They found that and more when they <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/bears-sign-seahawks-coby-bryant-safety-nfl" target="_blank" >agreed to sign</a> the Seahawks' <b>Coby Bryant</b>. He'll get a three-year, $40 million deal, per ESPN. Bryant, who had four interceptions last year, can play both free safety and strong safety. It's unclear if his additions means Kevin Byard's Bears tenure is over. Both sides had been optimistic about getting a deal done for Byard, who mostly played free safety.</p><h3><b>March 8</b></h3><p>Defensive end <b>Daniel Hardy</b>, who has been a special teams ace, agreed to sign a two-year deal worth as much as $6 million to return to the Bears, a source confirmed to the Sun-Times on Sunday. Hardy played only 5% of the Bears' defensive snaps last year but appeared in a whopping 78% of their kicking plays, totaling 22 tackles.</p><h3><b>March 6</b></h3><p>The day <b>Drew Dalman</b> put in his retirement paperwork, the Bears traded a 2027 fifth-round draft pick to the Patriots for his replacement, center <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/06/bears-trade-garrett-bradbury-patriots-center-2027-fifth-round-pick-nfl" target="_blank" ><b>Garrett Bradbury</b></a>. Bradbury, who started every game for the AFC champions last year, has one year left on a $9.6 million contract he signed last year after spending six years with the Vikings.</p><h3><b>March 5</b></h3><p>Needing to clear cap space and cognizant of their depth at wide receiver, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/halas-intrigue/2026/03/05/halas-intrigue-podcast-breaking-down-bears-dj-moore-trade-nfl" target="_blank" >the Bears traded</a> <b>DJ Moore</b> and a fifth-round pick to the Bills for a second-round draft pick this year. Moore jad just 682 receiving yards on 50 catches last year, the worst numbers of his career. Had the Bears kept Moore, he would have cost a $24.5 million salary cap hit, the third-highest on the team.</p><p>Eight days after telling linebacker <b>Tremaine Edmunds</b> and his agent that they could seek a trade, the Bears told Edmunds <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/05/bears-release-tremaine-edmunds-linebacker-salary-cap-nfl" target="_blank" >they were cutting him</a>. They saved $15 million in cap space in the process. Edmunds had four interceptions in 13 games last year, the second-most among all NFL linebackers.</p><h3><b>March 3</b></h3><p>Center <b>Drew Dalman</b>, who played every snap and reached the Pro Bowl in his first year with the Bears, told the team he planned to retire after just five years in the NFL. He filed his retirement paperwork three days later.</p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><bsp-list-loadmore class="PageListH" data-module data-hide-authors="true" data-hide-categories="true" entries.author: Jason Lieser

entries.title: Council committee backs Susan Cappello for city's Animal Care and Control chief
entries.summary: <p>A City Council committee on Tuesday advanced Mayor <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/city-hall/2026/02/25/mayor-brandon-johnson-animal-care-and-control-susan-cappello" >Brandon Johnson’s choice</a> to lead Chicago’s overburdened and under-resourced Commission on Animal Care and Control, despite strong objections from volunteers and animal welfare organizations.</p><p>Susan Cappello spent three years as acting executive director of the city-run shelter at 2741 S. Western Ave. before Johnson chose her for the permanent job.</p><p>That hasn't stopped 27 animal welfare organizations and scores of shelter volunteers from condemning Cappello’s leadership and demanding that the Johnson administration conduct a nationwide search for her replacement.</p><p>They described a toxic environment of “hostility, manipulation, favoritism and retaliation” where volunteers who complain are summarily dismissed. They raised concerns about overcrowded and unsanitary shelter conditions, where dogs are held in cages, sometimes in their own waste — and taken for a walk often just once a week. </p><p>“Have you seen the conditions? Have you seen that recently, they started using puppy pee pads on the counters because there are so many rodents that… they have to throw out the treats they buy from their own pockets?” said Heather Owen, co-founder of One Tail-at-a-Time, a Chicago-based animal rescue organization.</p><p>Nevertheless, the City Council’s Committee on Health and Human Relations approved Cappello’s appointment on a voice vote. That sets the stage for a full Council vote next week. </p><p> Kelly Gandurski, who ran the city shelter under former Mayors Rahm Emanuel and Lori Lightfoot, asserted that the problems at Animal Care and Control are “not Sue Cappello problems." They are "community problems" in a city that earmarks just $8 million a year for animal care and control, less than one third than the average spent in other major cities. </p><p>The spending shortage comes at a time when the number of dogs and cats arriving at the city-run shelter has surged by nearly 5,000 over the last two years. That's because Chicagoans who can no longer afford to support themselves are choosing to <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/money/2025/07/25/pet-owner-surrender-dogs-cats-overwhelming-shelters-rescues" >give up their pets</a> to a shelter that turns no animal away.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-b50000" name="image-b50000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6b2ca93/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5936x3331+0+313/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa5%2F6d%2F41de7a3b4e4da5610219f2fc1dc9%2Fmayorpets-260226-22.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/37985a7/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5936x3331+0+313/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa5%2F6d%2F41de7a3b4e4da5610219f2fc1dc9%2Fmayorpets-260226-22.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/d9dbdba/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5936x3331+0+313/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa5%2F6d%2F41de7a3b4e4da5610219f2fc1dc9%2Fmayorpets-260226-22.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="A man plays with a cat at Chicago Animal Care & Control on Wednesday, Feb. 25, 2026. " srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/d9dbdba/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5936x3331+0+313/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa5%2F6d%2F41de7a3b4e4da5610219f2fc1dc9%2Fmayorpets-260226-22.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/ebd6871/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5936x3331+0+313/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa5%2F6d%2F41de7a3b4e4da5610219f2fc1dc9%2Fmayorpets-260226-22.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/d9dbdba/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5936x3331+0+313/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa5%2F6d%2F41de7a3b4e4da5610219f2fc1dc9%2Fmayorpets-260226-22.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>A man plays with a cat at Chicago Animal Care & Control Feb. 25. </p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Giacomo Cain/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>“CACC is being asked to do the impossible. Never stop taking animals in, avoid euthanasia and somehow place animals with rescue groups and adopters that are already overwhelmed. This is not a management failure. This is a systemic problem," Gandurski said Tuesday.</p><p>Gandurski argued that Chicago has long had a "severe animal over-population issue, particularly with pit bull-mix dogs that are often bred in backyards, sold cheaply without vaccines or proper care and then end up in a shelter a year or two later with medical or behavioral issues."</p><p>"What CACC needs is not finger-pointing. It needs legislative action” to ban backyard breeding and reduce the flow of animals into the shelter, she said.</p><p>WGN Radio’s Steve Dale, a certified animal behavior consultant, said he has seen “nearly four decades of executive directors come and go” amid controversy. But Dale said he has never seen "vitriol" like the coordinated campaign against Cappello. </p><p>“The attacks are relentless, hurled like scoops of dog--- I won’t say the word,” Dale said.</p><p></p><p>Even though animal intake has been surging, Cappello said, transfers to the city’s rescue partners have risen to their highest levels in a decade. Euthanasia rates remained flat from 2024 to 2025, when adoption rates rose to the highest in department history.</p><p>“I recognize that concerns have been raised about shelter operations, conditions and outcomes and I take those concerns seriously,” Cappello told committee members. “Municipal animal sheltering is complex work and the decisions our staff face every day are often difficult.”</p><p>Cappello said her chief priorities going forward are to strengthen the dangerous dog ordinance banning backyard breeding that stalled under Gandurski, and ensure that every dog gets walked every day instead of once a week.</p><p>She also vowed to promptly fill the 17 new jobs bankrolled by the $657,000 increase in her annual budget.</p><p>“We’re adding more shelter cleaning staff… We have more animal control officers… More dispatch clerks to help really put together something similar to like a call center so that we are calling residents back on their service requests in a timely manner, and then a program specialist to help us… look into best practices elsewhere,” she said.</p><p></p><p><sup></sup></p><p></p><p>Animal Care and Control has long been the revolving door of city government. In 2018, Gandurski replaced an executive director fired for warehousing dogs in conditions that made dangerous dogs more dangerous. Five years earlier, then-Inspector General Joe Ferguson concluded that Chicago’s lost, stray and impounded dogs and cats were not always cleaned and fed properly — or getting veterinary exams within 24 hours — because of a 30% vacancy rate in feeding and cleaning staff.</p>
entries.author: Fran Spielman

entries.title: Marlene Leone, who made party dreams come true at head of catering at Chicago's Ritz-Carlton, dies at 83
entries.summary: <p></p><p></p><p></p><p>Marlene Leone had stories to tell.</p><p>As head of catering at the Ritz-Carlton, Chicago for decades she coordinated glitzy gatherings of all sorts. </p><p>There was the time her meticulously planned dinner featuring Bill Clinton was thrown into chaos because the famously chatty former president ran into U2 frontman Bono in the lobby of the Ritz and bent his ear for more than a minute, throwing the timing of the whole affair out of whack.</p><p>She was an unflappable presence at weddings, corporate events, charity galas and birthdays.</p><p>She once saw to it that a pony made its way to the hotel's 12th floor ballroom to help celebrate a bat mitzvah.</p><p>Chef George Bumbaris, who co-owns Prairie Grass Cafe in Northbrook but formerly worked at the Ritz, remembers a birthday party for Oprah Winfrey in which he served sorbet in the middle of a piece of ice shaped like an "O" for about 350 people.</p><p>Bumbaris, who marveled he was able to pull off the specialty desserts, noted the extravagant spending on plants, trees and flowers that went into transforming the space for the event into a garden scene.</p><p>"She produced some great venues, unbelievable the stuff," he said.</p><p>"We used to view it kind of like show business, and Marlene would write the script along with whoever was throwing the party," said George Anderson, former banquets manager at the Ritz.</p><p>"If it was a client's dream, she made it happen," recalled Marc Kaufman, who worked for Ms. Leone at the Ritz for years. "And Marlene always kept her cool," he added.</p><p>Ms. Leone was one of the first people hired at the Ritz when it opened in Chicago in 1975. At the time, the ultra-high-end hotel had little competition in that market in Chicago.</p><p>Ms. Leone helped set trends that morphed into industry standards — like allowing guests to choose entrees at the table, Kaufman said.</p><p>"She's an icon in catering world," said Joe Monastero, who works for the Texas Restaurant Association and counts Ms. Leone as a mentor. "You know the song 'Puttin' on the Ritz?' ... That is Marlene."</p><p>Ms. Leone, who retired in 2003, died March 2 at a senior living community in Evanston from natural causes. She was 83.</p><p>"Her career was a central part of her," said her daughter Johna Hedden, noting that her mother regularly worked 15-hour days.</p><p>"Sometimes I'd go to the hotel as a kid, and there'd be the most amazing group of people who were immersed and committed to the mission, from the linen providers and lighting people and the florist to the waiters in the kitchen who used to pretend to hold me over vats of soup," she said with a laugh.</p><p>She was one of the first women, if not the first, to run catering for a high-end hotel downtown, colleagues said.</p><p>"It's all the more impressive when you consider she didn't have a college education but was regularly hiring young people with master's degrees in hospitality management from places like Cornell University," said her daughter.</p><p>Ms. Leone was born June 7, 1942, in Cleveland, Ohio, to Joe and Lee Leone. He built swimming pools, and she was a homemaker.</p><p>Ms. Leone worked part time at the Cleveland Sheraton in high school and worked at a hotel in Washington D.C., before moving to Chicago.</p><p>She worked at the Seven Eagles, a now-shuttered restaurant and banquet hall in Des Plaines, and was part of the staff that opened the first hotel at O'Hare International Airport, which later became the O'Hare Hilton.</p><p>In 1997 she received a lifetime achievement award from the Catering Executives Club of America.</p><p>"For the longest time she didn't want to talk about her accomplishments as a woman in the industry, because she'd been so focused for so long on transcending that, and being who she needed to be to make it. But her contributions in leading the way for other women were highlighted that night and I'm glad they were because it took her until that moment to relax into that part of her legacy," her daughter said.</p><p>"She came from humble beginnings, she learned kitchen French and taught herself food etiquette and how to become this person who could play at this echelon, and I'm sure there was some impostor syndrome that she had to overcome, but, really, she was a natural at it and at the end of the day it was her: unbroken and unbreakable competency and poise and sophistication," she said.</p><p>In addition to her daughter, Ms. Leone is survived by her grandson, Jaden Hedden.</p><p>A funeral Mass will be held March 28 at 11:30 a.m. at Our Lady of Perpetual Help church in Glenview.</p>
entries.author: Mitch Dudek

entries.title: Jets to sign former Bears CB Nahshon Wright
entries.summary: <p>Nashshon Wright, who led the NFL in takeaways as a member of the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears" target="_blank" >Bears</a> last year, won’t be coming back to Halas Hall.</p><p>He agreed to sign a one-year, $5.5 million contact to join the Jets, NFL Network reported Tuesday.</p><p>Wright was the great surprise of the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/10/bears-2026-nfl-draft-order-set-first-second-third-fourth-round-ryan-poles-ben-johnson-pittsburgh" target="_blank" >Bears</a>’ season. One year after he failed to play a single defensive down for the Vikings, Wright signed with the Bears as a depth piece and to reunite with defensive backs coach Al Harris. Jaylon Johnson’s injury on the eve of training camp gave Wright the chance to play meaningful snaps in training camp and he impressed the coaching staff. He wound up starting all but one game during the regular season.</p><p>Wright finished with five interceptions, one returned for a touchdown — a 74-yarder in the Bears' season opener — and a whopping three fumble recoveries. He forced two fumbles. He did both when he punched the ball out of Jalen Hurts’ arms during a “Tush Push” against the Eagles on “Black Friday.” It was one of the highlights of the Bears’ season.</p><p>Wright was upset that he wasn’t named to the Pro Bowl when the votes were announced, but he was added to the NFC team as an injury replacement.</p><bsp-list-loadmore class="PageListH" data-module data-hide-authors="true" entries.author: Patrick Finley

entries.title: Jets trade for new starter to replace former Bear Justin Fields
entries.summary: <p>A year ago, the Jets thought their found their answer at quarterback in former <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears" target="_blank" >Bears</a> first-round pick Justin Fields.</p><p>Tuesday, they traded for a new starter.</p><p>The Jets traded a sixth-round pick for the Raiders’ Geno Smith and a seventh-rounder, locking in a reunion with the man who last played for them nine years ago. The plan is for Smith to be the starter.</p><p>The Jets figure to cut or trade Fields, who has one year and $20 million left on his contract. He has $10 million guaranteed for this upcoming year.</p><p>Moving Fields would end a tumultuous year for both the quarterback and the Jets. The former started only nine games, winning two of them, for the 3-14 Jets. He went `128-for-204 for 1,259 yards, seven touchdowns and one interception. It was clear by the end of the season the Jets would look elsewhere for their quarterback.</p><p>The quarterback was ripped by owner Woody Johnson after the Jets started 0-7. Johnson said that “if we can just complete a pass, it would look good,” and said that “something is not just jiving” with Fields.</p><p>Fields was benched midgame and received supportive text messages from his sister and stepmom the following week. That support, when he said he needed it most, left Fields crying on the floor of his closet. He started the next game and beat the Bengals, 39-38.</p><p>The <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/10/bears-2026-nfl-draft-order-set-first-second-third-fourth-round-ryan-poles-ben-johnson-pittsburgh" target="_blank" >Bears</a> traded up to draft Fields 11<sup>th</sup> overall in 2021. The following year, he ran for 1,143 yards, the second-most ever for a quarterback. Bears general manager Ryan Poles decided to keep Fields for a third season and trade the 2023 No. 1 overall pick to the Panthers. When the Panthers finished last in the NFL to give the Bears the top choice, Poles dealt Fields to the Steelers so he could draft Caleb Williams.</p><p>Fields started six games for the Steelers in 2024 before losing his starting job to Russell Wilson.</p>
entries.author: Patrick Finley

entries.title: Abogados de Chicago lideran esfuerzo para nombrar a fiscal especial que investigue a agentes de inmigración
entries.summary: <p>Una prestigiosa firma de abogados de Chicago que luchó contra el Operativo Midway Blitz en la corte federal anunció el lunes un plan para presionar por el nombramiento de un fiscal especial que investigue y potencialmente acuse a los funcionarios de la ley que llevaron a cabo la agresiva campaña de deportación en el área de Chicago.</p><p>La firma, Loevy and Loevy, dijo que 200 individuos y organizaciones están apoyando una petición para instalar a un externo en la oficina de la Fiscal del Estado del Condado de Cook, Eileen O’Neill Burke, quien ha pasado semanas diciendo que su personal sólo puede llegar hasta cierto punto para dirigirse a los federales.</p><p>La coalición planea llevar a cabo una conferencia de prensa el jueves por la tarde en Federal Plaza, en el centro, indicó Loevy and Loevy en un comunicado.</p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-d30000" name="module-d30000"></a> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody"><h3>La Voz Chicago WhatsApp</h3>Encuentra más noticias en nuestro canal de WhatsApp. <a class="Link" href="https://www.whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb0XT1XCHDylm3rCs40P" target="_blank" ><b>Síguenos.</b></a></div> </div><p>O’Neill Burke rechazó el llamado por un fiscal especial, calificando cualquier petición de “frívola [y] contraria a siglos de precedentes legales y fallos judiciales”.</p><p>“El plan reportado de solicitar un fiscal especial dificultará la capacidad de la Oficina de la Fiscal del Estado del Condado de Cook para procesar y obtener condenas contra los agentes del [Servicio de Inmigración y Control de Aduanas (ICE)] que rompen la ley y asegurar justicia para las víctimas perjudicadas por su conducta”, escribió O’Neill Burke en un comunicado.</p><p>“Los riesgos son demasiado altos como para equivocarnos, y me opondré enérgicamente a esta petición”.</p><p>En su comunicado, Loevy and Loevy destacó la fuerza extrema utilizada por los funcionarios federales de inmigración durante la campaña de deportación de varios meses, refiriéndose a las acciones en servicio como “crímenes”. Citó el uso generalizado de gas lacrimógeno, ataques contra "civiles pacíficos" y tiroteos que hirieron a <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz/2026/02/12/afirma-agente-patrulla-fronteriza-suspendido-dispararle-mujer-chicago" target="_blank" >Marimar Martínez</a> y mataron a <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz/2025/11/18/silverio-villegas-gonzalez-asesinado-por-agente-de-ice-en-area-de-chicago-tenia-cocaina-en-el-sistema-autopsia" target="_blank" >Silverio Villegas González</a>.</p><p>“Sin embargo, no ha habido investigación ni acusación federal o estatal contra ninguno de los individuos que perpetraron estos delitos”, dijo la firma. “Un fiscal especial e independiente es esencial para asegurar que haya responsabilidad en el Condado de Cook”.</p><p>Las demandas para que la oficina de O’Neill Burke investigue a los agentes federales no son nada nuevo. De hecho, otro abogado ya presentó una demanda a principios de este año buscando instalar a un fiscal especial para manejar tales casos en la corte del condado.</p><p>O’Neill Burke ha mantenido que su oficina está limitada en su capacidad para procesar y asegurar condenas contra los agentes federales en servicio. Aun así, el mes pasado <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2026/02/19/chicago-ice-crime-border-patrol-trump-midway-blitz-alex-pretti-renee-good-eileen-oneill-burke" target="_blank" >publicó una guía</a> que muestra cómo podría presentar cargos si un caso se lleva a su oficina.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-fad19a" name="image-fad19a"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/ea5f3b1/2147483647/strip/true/crop/7312x4104+0+387/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F03%2F13%2Fa487f2ae407db04482883ab2e9ef%2Fstatesatty-120324-16.jpg width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/7c6a1f7/2147483647/strip/true/crop/7312x4104+0+387/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F03%2F13%2Fa487f2ae407db04482883ab2e9ef%2Fstatesatty-120324-16.jpg"
